title: Equivalent fractions
description: Practice making equivalent fractions by multiplying the numerator and denominator by the same number.
topic_path: Module 5: Fraction equivalence, ordering, and operations
Topic B: Fraction equivalence using multiplication and division

hints:
We can start with a fraction and change the way we divide the whole to make an **equivalent fraction.**

Equivalent fractions have the same area as each other.

This square has $\blueE{2}$ equal parts, with $\maroonD{1}$ part shaded. It represents $\dfrac{\maroonD{1}}{\blueE{2}}$.

 ![fraction with 1 of 2 equal parts shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4bdbb842e6cce79652deb3d8d155493da1173fdb)

We can split up the whole square in a different way but keep the same area.

 ![fraction with the same area with 2 of 4 equal parts shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/996eb6d091359626c22baa57e1851bbe3260aa6c)

This square has $\maroonD{p}$ shaded parts, with $\blueE{4}$ equal parts. It represents $\dfrac{\maroonD{p}}{\blueE{4}}$.

How many shaded  parts are in the square?
There are $\maroonD{2}$ shaded parts in the square, so $\maroonD{p}=\maroonD{2}$.
$\maroonD{p}=\maroonD{2}$
